questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: The Performance Grade Index (PGI) 2.0 draws data from which of the following sources?

options.text: UDISE+, National Achievement Survey (NAS), and PM-POSHAN portal only
UDISE+, Annual Status of Education Report (ASER), and PRABAND portal only
UDISE+, NAS, PM-POSHAN portal, PRABAND portal, and Vidyanjali Portal
NAS, Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), and Vidyanjali Portal

questions.explanation: PGI 2.0 utilizes multiple data sources for its 73 indicators, including UDISE+, NAS, PM-POSHAN, PRABAND, and Vidyanjali. It does not use ASER (which is a private citizen-led survey) or PLFS.
questions.takeaway: PGI 2.0 relies on a diverse set of official government portals and national surveys to ensure a holistic assessment of school education.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Data sources: Unified District Information System for Education Plus (UDISE+), National Achievement Survey (NAS), PM-POSHAN portal, PRABAND portal and Vidyanjali Portal.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: According to the recent data on Indian school education progress, which of the following areas is categorized as showing 'Slow Progress'?

options.text: Gender gap in completion rate for upper secondary education
Access to girls' toilets in schools

questions.explanation: The report distinguishes between areas of fast and slow progress. Fast progress was noted in the out-of-school rate for primary children. Slow progress was noted in the out-of-school rate for lower secondary and the gender gap in completion rate for upper secondary.
questions.takeaway: While primary education metrics are improving rapidly, secondary education completion and gender parity remain challenges.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Fast Progress: In improving out-of-school rate of primary children. Slow Progress: In Out-of-school rate (lower secondary), and Gender gap in completion rate (upper secondary).
